course_title: Refugees, Asylum Seekers and the Law: Rights and Realities

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate a thorough understanding of the legal and institutional framework of refugee protection; notably international refugee law, and human rights law.
Understand and critically appreciate the definition of a 'refugee' in international law, and in regional legal instruments.
Understand and appreciate refugee status determination processes.
Understand and critically appreciate the situation, needs, and legal rights/interests of asylum seekers and refugees under national and international law.
Identify, structure and discuss relevant contemporary socio-legal issues and challenges to refugee protection, and present them logically and effectively.
Develop alternative strategies for the regulation and protection of refugees and asylum seekers, including recommendations for law reform and policy change.
Present and communicate socio-legal knowledge, understanding and critical awareness orally (to an audience of peers) and in writing.

assessments.task_description:
The first assessment will be released to students in week five of the semester.

Details of the first summative assessment (3000-word essay) and assessment/marking criteria (rubric) will be circulated to the cohort and discussed in seminars. An assessment rubric provides a framework which clarifies the assessment requirements and the standards of performance for different grade bands.

The assessment examines legal material and topics covered in the first four seminars via the formative tasks set each week (in weeks 2-5).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments must be submitted electronically via the online assessment submission link on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Oral Assessment - Access to refugee protection
assessments.task_description: The oral assessment will require students to demonstrate their understanding and critical awareness of several (related) themes (or topics) considered in the course in seminars five through to eight, carefully informed by the prescribed reading materials for those seminars and further independent study. After teaching and learning in this course finishes (week eleven) students are afforded over four weeks to revise and prepare for the oral assessment in the course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The oral presentation for each students will be scheduled during the examination period 16 - 20 November, at a time that avoids conflicts with other assessment deadlines or examinations.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 11(03 Aug - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Seminars

This course will cover the following topics:

  • Week 2 - Intro: Refugee Protection and International Law
  • Week 3 - Who is a Refugee (I)? Questions of Inclusion
  • Week 4 - Who is a Refugee (II)? Questions of Inclusion
  • Week 5 - Who is a Refugee (III)? Exclusion from Protection (on National Security or Criminal Grounds)
  • No seminar weeks 6 & 7
  • Week 8 - Access to Asylum: Third Country Processing
  • Week 9 - Access to Asylum: Maritime Interdiction at Sea
  • Week 10 - The Complexity of Refugee Status Determination
  • Week 11 - Forms of Refugee Protection and Settlement

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
